chapters.title: Chord Fundamentals and Chord Exercises (Lessons 1-5)
chapters.notes: Part One opens. Lesson 1 introduces the 26 essential chords; Lessons 2-4 work chord exercises chromatically up the neck and substitute new chords for standard ones in F, G, Ab, Bb. Lesson 5 is review and transposition practice.

chapters.title: Melody Chord Progressions, Introductions, Arpeggio and String Bass Styles (Lessons 6-8)
chapters.notes: Lesson 6 begins melody chord progressions with simple introductions in G major. Lesson 7 extends to F, Ab, Bb. Lesson 8 demonstrates arpeggio and string bass styles applied to Intro No. 2.

chapters.title: Chords in C/Db/Eb, Harmonic Structure and Chord Progressing (Lessons 9-12)
chapters.notes: Lesson 9 introduces chord exercises in C, Db, Eb major. Lesson 10 covers harmonic structure of chords (major/minor/dominant adaptable substitutions). Lesson 11 maps standard-to-new chord progressing charts. Lesson 12 explains substitution principles (5th of dominant = relative minor).

chapters.title: Vamps and Fill-ins (Lessons 13-14)
chapters.notes: Lesson 13 introduces vamps for jump and novelty tunes in C major with transposition guidance. Lesson 14 adds chords 27-30 and vamp ideas as fill-ins behind horn players, worked in G major.

chapters.title: Bridges and Last Lesson in Chord Progressions (Lessons 15-16)
chapters.notes: Lesson 15 presents three most-used jazz bridges in C major. Lesson 16, framed as 'the key to all of the other lessons,' covers dominant-to-dominant, dominant-to-minor, and dominant-to-major chord connections.

chapters.title: Applying Chords, Introductions and Endings (Lessons 17-18)
chapters.notes: Lesson 17 directs the student to apply learned chords to popular tunes. Lesson 18 collects introductions, endings, and modern guitarist chord tricks meant to be transposed and combined with any melody.

chapters.title: Chords with Two Names, Intros, Bop Blues, Endings and Sequences (Lessons 19-23)
chapters.notes: Lesson 19 analyzes chords with two names (Gma6/Emi7, etc.) and adds new chords. Lessons 20-22 cover intros, bop blues progressions, and endings in G major. Lesson 23 develops 8-bar and 16-bar sequences, closing Part One.

chapters.title: Part Two Opens: Time Values, Wrist Development and Scales (Lessons 24-26)
chapters.notes: Part Two begins on page 27 with the author's transition statement. Lesson 24 covers time values and exercises; Lesson 25 wrist development; Lesson 26 scales and exercises foundational to the run-based melodic vocabulary that follows.

chapters.title: Scale Runs, Minor/Dominant/Diminished Runs and Blues Pattern (Lessons 27-31)
chapters.notes: Lesson 27 scale runs in F and Db position; Lesson 28 minor 7th runs; Lesson 29 dominant 7th and 9th runs; Lesson 30 diminished runs; Lesson 31 the blues pattern, presenting the core run vocabulary.

chapters.title: Review, Major/Minor/Dominant Runs, Run Connections and Blues Solos (Lessons 32-37)
chapters.notes: Lessons 32-33 review and practice. Lesson 34 covers major, minor, and dominant runs. Lesson 35 run connections in G major. Lesson 36 a blues solo. Lesson 37 bounce blues in G major.

chapters.title: Riffs, Back to Vamp, Bridge Solo and Rhythm Changes (Lessons 38-43)
chapters.notes: Lesson 38 riffs in G major; Lesson 39 back to vamp; Lesson 40 bridge solos; Lessons 41-42 review and practice; Lesson 43 rhythm changes — the section applying scale-run vocabulary over standard forms.

chapters.title: Reviews, Solo Sketches, Riffs and Groove Riffs (Lessons 44-48)
chapters.notes: Lesson 44 review/practice; Lesson 45 solo sketches; Lesson 46 more solo sketches; Lesson 47 riffs in Bb major; Lesson 48 groove riffs. (No Lesson 46 heading appears in transcript between Lesson 45 and Lesson 47 — material runs continuously through pages 53-56.)

chapters.title: Building Around the Melody and Solo Patterns (Lessons 49-52)
chapters.notes: Lesson 49 review and practice; Lessons 50-51 building around the melody using 'Dark Eyes' as the worked example; Lesson 52 solo patterns, closing the book.
